context: Acme Industrial sells vibration monitoring sensors and predictive maintenance systems to automotive Tier 1 and Tier 2 suppliers. Their reps typically sell to plant managers and maintenance directors. Average deal size is $80K-$150K annually. Sales cycles run 3-6 months. Key differentiator is their proprietary sensor accuracy and on-site installation support.

BrightPath sells patient engagement and intake automation software to mid-size hospital systems and specialty clinic networks. Their reps call on CIOs, VP of Patient Experience, and clinic administrators. Average deal size $200K-$500K. They compete primarily against Epic's built-in modules and manual paper processes.

Summit sells a BI and analytics platform to mid-market companies (500-5000 employees). Their reps sell to VP of Data, CFOs, and heads of operations. Average ACV is $60K-$120K. Compete against Tableau, Looker, and the 'we'll just use Excel' crowd. Key differentiator is speed to insight and no-code setup.
